objective: Close honest-limit #2 of the qudit ladder: upgrade the outer wall from 'NPT-entangled' (negativity) to genuinely 'entangled' by adding the CCNR/realignment criterion, a bound-entanglement witness independent of the PPT test.
criterion: ||R(rho)||_1 > 1 => entangled, where R(rho)[(a a'),(b b')] = rho[(a b),(a' b')]. Sufficient, not necessary.

payoff.state: Tiles UPB bound-entangled state (Bennett et al., 3x3)
payoff.reading: PPT (negativity ~ 0, invisible to the old outer wall) but CCNR > 1 (caught by the upgraded wall).

effect_on_ladder: On the isotropic family PPT <=> separable, so CCNR and negativity give the SAME entanglement wall at F = 1/d. The qudit ladder result is UNCHANGED on the isotropic line; the upgrade only adds detection of bound entanglement off that line.
scope_limits: CCNR is sufficient, not necessary; PPT-entangled states it misses may remain. No claim-ledger movement.
